cards.text: A {{c1::pointer to const}} (const int* ptr) cannot change the value being pointed to. A {{c2::const pointer}} (int* const ptr) cannot change what it points to.

cards.front: What is the difference between 'const int* ptr' and 'int* const ptr'?
cards.back: 'const int* ptr' is a pointer to const (can't modify value). 'int* const ptr' is a const pointer (can't reassign pointer). Read right-to-left.

cards.question: Which lines cause errors?
cards.answer: Line 1 causes an error. Cannot modify value through pointer to const. Line 2 is OK - the pointer itself can be reassigned.
cards.explanation: Pointer to const means the pointed-to value is const, not the pointer itself.

cards.question: Which lines cause errors?
cards.answer: Line 2 causes an error. Cannot reassign a const pointer. Line 1 is OK - can modify value through non-const pointer.
cards.explanation: Const pointer means the pointer itself cannot be changed after initialization.

cards.text: A {{c1::const int* const ptr}} is both a const pointer and a pointer to const. Neither the pointer nor the value can be changed.

cards.explanation:
Read right-to-left to understand const with pointers:
  • const int* ptr: ptr is a pointer to const int
  • int* const ptr: ptr is a const pointer to int
  • const int* const ptr: const pointer to const int
const left of * applies to value, right of * applies to pointer.
examples: const int* p1;        // pointer to const int
int const* p2;        // same as above
int* const p3 = &x;   // const pointer to int
const int* const p4 = &x;  // const pointer to const int

cards.text: In const int* ptr, const applies to the {{c1::pointed-to value}}. In int* const ptr, const applies to the {{c2::pointer itself}}.
Read pointer declarations {{c1::right-to-left}}: int* const reads as 'const {{c2::pointer}} to int'.
